content_markdown: A room is at a constant temperature of $300\text{ K}$. A hotplate in the room is at a temperature of $400\text{ K}$ and loses energy by radiation at a rate of $P$. What is the rate of loss of energy from the hotplate when its temperature is $500\text{ K}$?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: The net rate of energy loss by radiation is governed by the Stefan-Boltzmann law, considering both emission and absorption: $P_{net} = e\sigma A(T^4 - T_{surroundings}^4)$. In the first case, $P \propto (400^4 - 300^4)$. In the second case, $P' \propto (500^4 - 300^4)$. By dividing the two expressions and factoring out the common $100^4$ terms, we find $P' = P \frac{5^4 - 3^4}{4^4 - 3^4}$.
